ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network event */,
  "protocol_type" INTEGER /* Type of network protocol */,
  "service" INTEGER /* Service associated with the network traffic */,
  "flag" TEXT /* Flags indicating specific network packet characteristics */,
  "src_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address */,
  "dst_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address */,
  "src_port" INTEGER /* Source port number */,
  "dst_port" INTEGER /* Destination port number */,
  "src_bytes" INTEGER /* Number of bytes sent from the source */,
  "dst_bytes" INTEGER /* Number of bytes sent to the destination */,
  "land" INTEGER /* Indicator of whether the connection is from/to a land-based network */,
  "classe" TEXT /* Class of the network traffic (e.g., normal, malicious) */,
  "posicaoOriginal" INTEGER /* Original position of the network traffic in the dataset */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the network traffic event */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the network traffic */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID linked to the traffic (if applicable) */,
  "threat_level" TEXT /* Threat level of the network traffic */,
  "geo_location_src" TEXT /* Geographical location of the source IP */,
  "geo_location_dst" TEXT /* Geographical location of the destination IP */,
  "anomaly_score" REAL /* Anomaly score indicating the likelihood of the traffic being anomalous */,
  "analysis_status" TEXT /* Status of the traffic analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_flag FOREIGN KEY ("flag") REFERENCES flags ("flag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_service FOREIGN KEY ("service") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_protocol_type FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_type") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the network protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the protocol supports encryption */,
  "max_packet_size" INTEGER /* Maximum packet size supported by the protocol */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the protocol entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the protocol entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "port" INTEGER /* Port number used by the service */,
  "is_secure" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the service is secure */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the service entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the service entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flags" (
  "flag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flag */,
  "flag_name" TEXT /* Name of the flag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the flag */,
  "is_syn" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the flag is a SYN flag */,
  "is_ack" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the flag is an ACK flag */,
  "is_fin" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the flag is a FIN flag */,
  "is_rst" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the flag is a RST flag */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the flag entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the flag entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, auditor) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user's information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., traffic, security, health) */,
  "generated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the report was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., pending, completed, error) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the generated report file */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or summary of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Event ID of the network traffic event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who detected the anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the anomaly was detected */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the anomaly */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly (e.g., new, under review, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("event_id")
)
